description.en: The purpose of the ontology is to model the domain of medical devices and in vitro diagnostic devices registered in the European EUDAMED database, including manufacturers, identified risks, incidents, and field safety corrective actions (FSCAs). The goal is to enable the different information systems in the sector — manufacturers, regulatory authorities, hospitals, and patients — to share data accurately, without ambiguity, and to interoperate easily with each other.

bibliographicCitation.value: Soler, Maria Isabel (2026). EUDAMED MDR-IVDR Ontology. Universitat Oberta de Catalunya (UOC).

comment.en: Regulatory zone covering all the European Union member states.
A group of countries where a corrective action applies. For example, EU-wide or EU-wide plus UK.
A notice sent to users with instructions on how to use or limit the device.
A possible problem related to the use of a medical device. Each risk has a type, a category, a severity, and a probability.
Medium probability that the risk happens.
Low probability that the risk happens.
High probability that the risk happens.
How likely a risk is to happen. The possible values are Low, Medium, or High.
A device that is both implantable and active. For example, a pacemaker.
An adverse event that happens with a medical device.
Risk of infection due to contamination or poor sterilization of the device.
Regulatory class of a medical device by risk level. It is defined by MDR (I, IIa, IIb, III) or by IVDR (A, B, C, D).
A corrective action done on devices that are already in use, without removing them from the market.
MDR risk class III. Highest risk for medical devices.
MDR risk class IIb. Moderate to high risk for medical devices.
MDR risk class IIa. Low to moderate risk for medical devices.
MDR risk class I. Lowest risk for medical devices.
Risk class for devices under MDR. The possible values are I, IIa, IIb, or III.
Left ventricular assist device used for end-stage heart failure. It is a centrifugal-flow pump that supports cardiac function in patients waiting for a heart transplant or as a long-term solution.
A medical device that needs an electrical source or another type of energy to work.
A device used to analyse samples from the human body, such as blood or urine.
Risk that the device or the patient gets contaminated.
Irish-American medical device company headquartered in Dublin. One of the largest medical device manufacturers in the world.
Roche Diagnostics is a Swiss multinational manufacturer of in vitro diagnostic devices and laboratory equipment, registered as an economic operator in EUDAMED.
A company or person that designs and produces medical devices. Manufacturers must register their devices in EUDAMED.
A medical device used only once on a single patient. Implantable devices are also single-use.
Risk of unauthorized access or attacks on connected medical devices.
Category of medical devices used only once on a single patient, such as drug-eluting balloons or disposable instruments.
Category of in vitro diagnostic devices that people can use at home, without medical staff, such as glucose monitors.
Category of medical devices used to analyse samples from the human body, such as blood, urine, or tissue.

labels.ca: Tipus Dispositiu de Diagnňstic In Vitro

comment.en: Category of medical devices that are placed inside the human body and stay there after the procedure, such as joint replacements or heart valves.
Category of medical devices that need an external energy source to work, such as imaging or monitoring equipment.
Category of medical devices that are both implantable and active, such as pacemakers or ventricular assist devices.
Closed enumeration of device type categories used to classify which devices a risk affects.
An agent that can report medical device incidents. It can be a manufacturer, an authority, a healthcare professional, or a patient.
An in vitro diagnostic device that people can use at home, without medical staff.
Safety alert issued by Medtronic in February 2024 to all centres implanting HeartMate 3 LVAD. The alert updates the anticoagulation protocol to reduce device thrombosis risk.

labels.en: Safety Alert for HeartMate 3 LVAD anticoagulation
labels.ca: Alerta de Seguretat per anticoagulació HeartMate 3 LVAD

comment.en: A message sent to users about a safety problem with a device.
Risk category related to the quality of the product.
Risk category related to the performance of the device.
Risk category related to the general safety of the device.
Risk category related to the device failing or stopping to work.
Risk category related to physical harm to the patient.
Risk category related to wrong diagnostic results.
General classification of a risk. It can be device failure, patient harm, performance, safety, diagnostic error, or product quality.
Risk of thrombus formation on the device surface, which can cause embolic events such as stroke. Critical for implantable cardiovascular devices.
Risk of physical harm to the patient caused by the device.
IVDR risk class B. Low to moderate risk for in vitro diagnostic devices.
IVDR risk class A. Lowest risk for in vitro diagnostic devices.
IVDR risk class D. Highest risk for in vitro diagnostic devices.
IVDR risk class C. Moderate to high risk for in vitro diagnostic devices.
Risk class for in vitro diagnostic devices under IVDR. The possible values are A, B, C, or D.
Country in Western Europe. Member State of the European Union.
Country in Central Europe. Member State of the European Union.
A country, used to identify where manufacturers are registered or where incidents are reported.
A general class for geographic areas. It includes both single countries and regulatory zones.
Male patient.
Female patient.
Sex of a patient involved in an incident. The possible values are Male or Female.
The corrective action has been completed.
The corrective action is still in progress.
Status of a corrective action. The possible values are Open or Closed.

Serious incident reported in Germany in January 2024. A patient implanted with a HeartMate 3 LVAD developed an ischemic stroke six months after implantation. Device thrombus was confirmed by echocardiography.
An incident that caused or could cause death or serious health problems.
Serious level of severity. The risk can cause important harm to the patient.
Moderate level of severity. The risk can cause limited harm to the patient.
Critical level of severity. The risk can cause death or very serious harm.
Level of how serious a risk is. The possible values are Critical, Serious, or Moderate.
Risk caused by radiation that comes from the device.
Risk caused by errors or bugs in the device software.
Risk caused by the materials used in the device, such as toxicity or wear.
Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation (EU) 2017/746.
The European regulation that controls a medical device. It can be MDR or IVDR.
An event that did not cause harm, but that could have been a serious incident.
Voluntary recall of Micra AV pacemaker units affected by premature battery depletion. The action was opened in February 2024 and closed in May 2024 after replacement of all affected devices.
A corrective action where the affected devices are removed from the market.
The Health Products Regulatory Authority is the competent authority for medical devices in Ireland.
The national authority in each EU country responsible for medical device regulation.

The cobas e 801 is an automated immunoassay analyzer used in clinical laboratories. It is an in vitro diagnostic device classified under the IVDR regulation. It performs immunological tests on patient samples without direct contact with the patient.
A device used for medical purposes such as diagnosis, prevention, or treatment.
Left ventricular assist device used for end-stage heart failure. It is a centrifugal-flow pump that supports cardiac function in patients waiting for a heart transplant or as a long-term solution.
A medical device that is placed inside the human body and stays there after the procedure.
A Field Safety Corrective Action. It is any action a manufacturer takes to reduce a risk, such as a recall, a safety alert, or a user notice.
Risk of wrong diagnostic results, such as false positives or false negatives.
Risk that the device gets disturbed by other devices, or that it disturbs other devices.
Risk that the device fails or stops working correctly.
Connects a risk to the types of medical devices that it can affect.
Date when the corrective action started.
Reference number of the corrective action in EUDAMED.
Email address used to contact the manufacturer.
Single Registration Number of the manufacturer in EUDAMED.
True if the device is implantable inside the human body.
Description of the scope of the corrective action, such as the affected lots or models.
Age of the patient involved in the incident.
Connects a corrective action to the incident that it addresses.
Indicates the EU regulation that applies to a device. It can be MDR or IVDR.
Connects a device to its regulatory risk class.
Date when the incident was detected.
True if the device needs an external energy source to work.
Connects a risk to its general category.
Indicates the sex of the patient involved in the incident.
Connects a risk to its severity level.
Reference number of the incident in EUDAMED.
Date when the incident was reported.
Date when the incident happened.
Date when the corrective action was closed.
True if the device is for in vitro diagnostic use.
Code from the IMDRF (International Medical Device Regulators Forum) used to classify the risk.
Connects an incident to the medical device involved.
Connects an incident to the risks that happened in it.
True if the device can be used more than once.
Text description of the entity, such as a risk, an incident, or a corrective action.
Authorization number assigned to a manufacturer.
The use for which the manufacturer designed the device.
Connects a risk to its probability level.
Description of what happened as a result of the incident.
Country where the incident was reported.
Geographic area affected by a corrective action. It can be a single country or a regulatory zone.
Connects a medical device to its manufacturer.
Unique Device Identifier - Device Identifier. It is the unique code of a medical device under MDR.
Connects a manufacturer to its competent authority.
True if a corrective action was taken after the incident.
Connects a manufacturer to its country of registration.
Connects an incident to the entity that reported it.
Number of devices affected by the corrective action.
Current status of a corrective action. It can be Open or Closed.
